overallInterpretation: The artifact is terminal output from a Vitest run of render-target.test.ts showing 10 passing tests across three groups: render target APIs, viewport APIs, and multi-pass rendering. The tests collectively demonstrate creating and tracking render targets, destroying them, setting a render target for offscreen rendering, restoring the browser framebuffer, binding a render target texture to a mesh material, setting viewport regions with normalized coordinates, resetting viewport to full window, split-screen viewport support, rendering to texture then to screen (nested view pattern), and cleanup of render targets on destroy. All 10 tests pass with zero failures.

artifacts.interpretation: Vitest verbose output showing 10/10 tests passing in 296ms. The tests cover all five capabilities described in the expected demo: (1) create a render target ('creates and tracks a render target'), (2) render to it ('sets render target for offscreen rendering', 'renders to texture then to screen'), (3) set viewport region ('sets viewport region', 'resets viewport to full window', 'supports split screen'), (4) bind texture to mesh material ('binds a render target texture to a mesh material'), and (5) clean up ('destroys a render target', 'supports cleanup of render targets on destroy').
